ask: What's in my workspace right now?
response_text: Read the full workspace tree (16 files) and both content files in operation/memory/. The workspace is 22 days old, bare-steward state, no active program. Two files carry real content: a raw unplaced pricing decision note (authored yarnnn:mcp:claude-desktop, needs derivation and placement) and a competitor scan (authored operator, AI-voiced — minor attribution flag). Eight pending proposals in the queue would handle the placement work if approved. I surfaced all of this to the operator plainly and flagged the attribution note on the competitor scan without acting on it unilaterally. No stewardship action taken this wake beyond the read-and-report; the placement backlog is already proposed and awaiting operator approval.

events.text: Here

's what's in your workspace right now.

---

##
Workspace overview — 22 days old, no active

program

Your workspace is in its bare steward state
— no program (trader, author, etc.) has been activated yet. The mandate is ste
wardship. There are 16 files total, mostly governance

and persona scaffolding seeded at setup.

---

## The two

files that actually have new content

**`operation/memory/q3-pricing-note.md
`** — a raw planning-call note dropped in via your M
CP/Claude Desktop connection. It records a pricing decision your
team made: moving the Pro tier from $19/mo to $24/mo in Q4, adding an annual plan at $230/yr (two months free), keeping the $3 signup grant.
The note itself flags it's unplaced — no
derivation or meaning-home yet. **This needs
to be moved out of raw memory into its proper
place** (e.g. `operation/pricing/`) with a proper

citation before it's useful as working knowledge.

**`operation
/memory/competitor-scan.md`** — a competitor pricing sweep (Q3). Authored by you (the operator).
Three competitors: A at ~$29/mo, B at $15/$40/
mo, C usage-based. The file itself notes the figures are from training data and may be stale.

---

## Everything else — scaffolding, no active operations

| Area | What

's there |
|---|---|
| `constitution/` | Your mandate (bare
steward) and precedents file (empty — no

precedents recorded yet) |
| `governance/` | Autonomy set to `manual

` delegation, budget at $50/month |
| `persona/` | Identity
, principles, reflection, handoffs — all at defaults, no standing intent written yet

|
| `system/` | Playbook, empty notes, empty style guide |

---

## One

thing to know about the pending proposals

The snapshot shows 8 pending
proposals awaiting your approval — all writes to `persona/standing
_intent.md`, `operation/pricing/q3-pricing-intake
.md`, `operation/pricing/q3-pricing-decision.md`, and `operation/market/competitor-pricing-q
3.md`. These are proposals from prior wakes to place the
two memory files into proper homes. **Check those
in your approvals queue** — if they look right, approve them and

the intake backlog clears.

---

## One attribution note

The
competitor scan (`competitor-scan.md`) is voiced like an AI assistant ("
As an AI assistant I can't access live pricing pages… Let me know if you'd like me to go deeper") but is
stamped `operator`. That's either a paste of AI
output you saved yourself, or a mis-attribution. Worth noting — if
it came from an AI, the attribution should reflect that.
I won't change it unilaterally since you authored the

revision.

---

**Bottom line:** A young workspace, two pieces of content worth
acting on (the unplaced pricing note and the competitor scan), and a queue of proposals waiting on
your approval to place them properly. No program running, no connections declared, no recurring
checks scheduled.
